examples.title: Algorithm: Calculate Mean
examples.description: Calculates the arithmetic average of a dataset
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateMean(data)
INPUT: data (array of numbers)
OUTPUT: mean (number)

BEGIN
 sum = 0
 FOR EACH value IN data
 sum = sum + value
 END FOR
 mean = sum / LENGTH(data)
 RETURN mean
END
examples.explanation: Step-by-step explanation of calculating the mean

examples.title: Python: Calculate Mean
examples.description: Calculates the arithmetic average of a dataset
examples.explanation: Calculates the mean by summing all values and dividing by the length of the dataset

examples.title: Python: Calculate Standard Deviation
examples.description: Calculates the standard deviation of a dataset
examples.code:
import math

def calculate_standard_deviation(data):
 mean = sum(data) / len(data)
 variance = sum((x - mean) ** 2 for x in data) / len(data)
 return math.sqrt(variance)

# Example usage:
data = [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]
std_dev = calculate_standard_deviation(data)
print(std_dev)
examples.explanation: Calculates the standard deviation by first calculating the variance and then taking the square root

examples.title: Python: Visualize Histogram
examples.description: Visualizes a histogram of a dataset
examples.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

data = [2, 4, 6, 8, 10]
plt.hist(data, bins=5)
plt.xlabel('Value')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')
plt.title('Histogram of Dataset')
plt.show()
examples.explanation: Creates a histogram with 5 bins and displays the frequency of each value in the dataset
